titre: For Demacia | Dev Update - League of Legends
transcription:
Hey everyone, I'm Paul, aka Pabro. And I'm Andrei, also known as Meddler. Welcome to Season One of 2026. We're back to talk more
about some of the things we have coming this Season, as well as a little bit
about some plans later in 2026 and beyond. First, though, let's quickly recap what
you can expect when you log in tomorrow. Whether you're playing Ranked, Norms, or Swiftplay,
you're in for some changes. Right off the bat, in those queues you’ll be
hitting your first minions faster, as we increased speeds in Champ Select and
the first minion and jungle spawns. We also adjusted some gameplay systems— like Faelights for expanded, temporary warding zones, 9 new and returning items, and a new Demolish-like mechanic on
towers called Crystalline Overgrowth. We’ve streamlined some objectives as well- removing Atakhan and his Blood Roses,
as well as Feats of Strength. And we’ve made the remaining Epic objectives,
like Baron and dragons, a bit stronger. Finally, we’re also adding a unique
role quest to top, mid, and bot lane, similar to the jungle and support quests
which are also getting some upgrades. Each lane will progress on those quests
by killing minions, taking towers, killing enemies, or helping
take down neutral objectives. And then once complete, these quests will
grant rewards based off your position. That means an increased level cap and
free Teleport spell for top laners, tier 3 boots and empowered recall for mid, or more gold and a seventh item slot for bot. There’s a lot more in store—including a whole
bunch of additional changes for Swiftplay. If you want a refresher, check out the dev blogs from
December that are linked in the description. All right, moving on. Let's talk a bit about what you can expect
going into Season One. As we shared in December, This year we wanted to kick things off
with unity, justice, and righteousness. And that means Demacia. But even more importantly that means... it’s Shyvana time y’all! As most of you probably saw
in yesterday's cinematic, she’s lookin’ pretty nifty with a new design. And after years of waiting, she’s finally getting
her update in Act II of this Season. Now, aside from updated visuals for her
base human and dragon forms, and of course all her skins, she’s got a Dr. Mundo or Volibear-level gameplay rework. At a high level,
her abilities remain pretty similar, but her finer kit details and gameplay
feel are meaningfully updated. We really wanted to focus on connecting
her two forms better throughout her kit. One of the clearest connections for
that is her ult. Shyvana still gains Fury when using abilities and taking down champions, cannons, and monsters. When she has enough Fury, she can cast her ult to transform into an actual dragon this time, and breathe down fire on everyone in her path. Another part of her kit that's pretty
similar to now is her passive: Shyvana will gain defensive stats and max Fury for taking down champions, cannons, and neutral monsters. Build-wise - we wanted to preserve her ability
to be played both as a bruiser or as a mage. That said, though, in terms of her core champion theme we want
her bruiser build to be the primary choice. It's the most natural feeling
one for most games. So we’ll prioritize that over being a poke mage. We want to save the rest of her kit for her full reveal in Act II, and we can't wait to see what you all think when you get your hands
or claws on her in a few patches. Summoner's Rift has a Demacia-themed
reskin with petricite towers, gold-winged accents,
and a few Easter eggs. There will also be a handful of motion
comics throughout the Season that will follow up on some of the moments
from the Season start cinematic. Outside of that, we also have a metagame that will be live
tomorrow called Demacia Rising. We're trying something different this time with a metagame that evolves
over the course of the Season, with updates
coming out at regular intervals. In Demacia Rising, you’ll experience the
rise of the Kingdom of Demacia through the eyes of a young Lux
as she gets lessons in history, politics and military strategy from her
aunt, Tianna Crownguard. You’ll help take Demacia from its humble
beginnings as a single small settlement, to a beautiful, shining nation. In Demacia Rising you’ll collect resources, unlock research, and recruit champions to grow your kingdom, all while fighting off invaders
and suppressing threats from within. Now, we took a lot of learnings
from previous metagames, and we wanted to make sure
you could play the way you wanted to. Rather than needing to say, dedicate 15
to 30 minutes at a time when engaging with a metagame like this. This time, you can spend as much
or as little time as you want. building and managing your cities, and you'll be able to complete fights
through auto battling. Now, if you choose to spend more time
playing, though, you'll be rewarded with some
bonus Battle Pass XP. And, finally, outside of Demacia-related things coming this Season, we also wanted to take a moment to discuss ARAM: Mayhem. It's been an incredibly popular mode
since it came out last year. Tons of you've been playing it,
and you've been telling us a lot about how much fun you're having
with all the wacky augments. So it's going to be sticking around
for a while longer. We’re also working on a larger update to ARAM: Mayhem that will come out in patch 26.03, but we'll share a bit more about that, though,
when we get closer to the time. Outside of thematic changes,
we have another change that we're trialing in some regions
starting tomorrow. Last year we were working on
really addressing game running behavior. You know, things like inting and griefing. And we have some other improvements
to cut down on dodging and lobby trolling, you know, like removing the ability to ban
your ally’s hovered champion or making it
so that autofill persists through dodges. We have another body of work that pairs with those two efforts:
lobby hostage intervention. Right now, there isn't a good way for us
to take immediate action on bad actors who hold lobbies hostage by threatening to run it down,
or just play something that doesn't work. Either you or someone else
is forced to dodge, or you have to play the game out, which means
more often than not, it ends in a loss. And while the perpetrator gets punished
and you get some LP refunded, your time isn't. So starting tomorrow in some regions,
you'll be able to terminate lobbies
that are held hostage by bad actors. We've been running some tests
on live servers for some time now. You know, using your lobby reports
to refine the system. And we have high confidence in that day
one accuracy. We're not going to explain
exactly how this works, because we don't want said bad actors
to try and find ways around the system, but we are going to look at a variety of
triggers, things like, you know, text intention. If somebody says, I'm
going to run it down. We're actually going
to trust them on that one. Or if they're doing something like
taking a Ghost / Cleanse on a champ in a lane they never play, same deal. If the lobby is terminated you'll be returned to the queue, similar
to how it works now when someone dodges. If you enjoy playing viable off
meta picks, don't need to worry about it. We still want experimentation
to thrive in League, so we'll be closely monitoring
how this system works with manual reviews. We'll be rolling out these changes to all regions slowly
to ensure that it remains high accuracy. Okay, and with all that said, we're
now going to hand things over to Riot Hylia and 100pc nuggets and they’ll talk a bit about some of the skins
we have coming this Season. Hi everyone. I'm 1,000,000pc nuggets. And I’m Riot Hylia. New year, same us. We’re back to talk to y’all about skins, art, and who’s going to be hitting the rift in Season One. As Pabro and Meddler said in December, we’re
going to have a mix of Demacian and non-Demacian skins to liven things up this Season. So for both our grab bag skins
and our larger lineups, we're reviving some beloved thematics
as well as doing some totally new ones. Starting off strong, next patch will have skins
for all our fellow anime lovers. First up we have Battle Academia Briar and Samira,
alongside our new stylized take on mecha anime with a skin for Ornn
and a new Legendary skin for Galio. I am so excited for Aegis Frame Galio. The team has done an amazing job
and he is looking so cool in-game! So good, I can't wait! Oh and of course we'll also be celebrating
Lunar Revel this year. When we were deciding what skins to make,
we had a few different ideas. We could bring back something that we haven't done in a while,
or we could try something new entirely. And honestly, we couldn't decide. So why not both? Yep. Firecracker will be returning this year,
bringing a fun, celebratory and upbeat style to the holiday
with lots of, well, Firecrackers. Firecrackers. And then we'll have a new thematic
that we're calling Petals of Spring that we made in collaboration
with Wild Rift. Petals of Spring was inspired by the beautiful elegance
of plum blossoms against snowy fields. We really wanted something
that could herald the new Season of spring with that high beauty, just to make sure we had some tonal
contrast from the fun of Firecracker. But if you're looking for something
a little goofier, don't worry, we have some pretty unhinged ideas
for April Fools this year. We certainly do. Like last year, we aren't going with a
single overarching theme for April Fools, and instead we're making skins based on
what works well for each champion. And we were inspired by a pretty wide
range of things like balloon animals, pugs, stonks, to name a few. Later in Act Two, we'll see a darker
side of Demacia with a new set of skins, and we've also got a totally new thematic
we're introducing for First Stand this year. It's a brand new take
on the established Road Warrior theme, where we're reimagining it in
a supernaturally-fueled-engine-powered fantasy with more edge, leather, chrome,
and maybe a little bit of werewolves. Just a little bit. Just enough. Yeah. All right. Outside of regular skin drops, we wanted
to take a moment to discuss some lists. So first: Prestige skins. Prestige mother Morgana will be out tomorrow. And then later this Season, Sona and Mordekaiser will also be receiving Prestige skins. Outside of Prestige skins, we also have a
Mythic Variant for Aurelion Sol coming next patch. And later this Season, Master Yi will be getting one as well. And finally,
let's talk about Exalted skins. Last year we mentioned that Viego would be receiving one
and then that he was delayed. But now it's finally time. Next patch Revenant Reign Viego will come to The Sanctum. We worked with a ton of Viego mains
to talk about what the ultimate Viego experience is,
and it became pretty obvious. Killing your enemies
and taking over their bodies. Oh, and having a huge sword. Right, the sword. Yeah. Iconic. Yeah. So with that in mind, we narrowed
in on a combination of those two things. So Revenant Reign Viego collects
swords from his enemies, gaining power and unique visual effects
for every class of enemy he possesses. There are a ton of cool moments
and features in the skin that we cannot wait for y'all to see. So keep your eyes peeled for the trailer
that drops later today. And finally, we wanted to let you know
that the Victorious skin this Season will be everyone's
favorite mustachioed muscle man: Braum. As a reminder, you can get the skin at any
rank as long as you play enough games. All right, well, that's it for us. We'll hand things over
to the esports team, who've got some updates on their plans
for the year. Thanks so much for watching
and we'll be back soon. Bye! Hi, everyone. I’m Whalen, Head of International Publishing & Esports and I'm Chris, Head of League of Legends Esports. We're incredibly excited to welcome you to the start of the 2026
League of Legends Esports Season. 2025 was a historic year for us, filled
with unforgettable storylines, big changes to the format,
and incredible competition. And of course, a huge congratulations
once again to T1, who made history by becoming the first team ever to win
three consecutive World Championships. What an unbelievable accomplishment
for them and for all the fans who followed their journey. Now, as we turn the page to a new year,
we're ready to kick off another incredible Season, one that builds on
everything we've learned, celebrates our global community, and continues
pushing LoL Esports forward. Let's dive into what's ahead for 2026 and some key updates
shaping the future of LoL Esports. And what better way to begin the Season
than with our first global event of the year: First Stand. From March 16 to 22, we’ll be bringing First Stand to São Paulo, Brazil, at the Riot Games Arena. Brazil has always been home to some of
the most passionate fans in the world, and we couldn't be more excited to bring
this event to the heart of CBLOL country. With the LCS and CBLOL
returning as independent leagues, the competition will now feature eight teams battling for early Season
dominance and regional pride. We'll be welcoming two teams from the LCK,
two from the LPL and one each from the LEC, LCS,
CBLOL, and LCP. Every match will be best of five,
which means more games, more Silver Scrapes,
and more opportunities for teams to show their skill, adaptability
and their creativity under pressure. And that's just the beginning of
what's ahead for our 2026 global events. After First Stand wraps up in Brazil,
we'll head into the Mid-Season Invitational hosted this year in
Daejeon, South Korea, marking the first time we've ever brought
MSI to this incredible city. We'll have more to share
on the venue soon, but we couldn't be more excited to bring one of our biggest international events
to a new stage in South Korea. And as we head into the new Season
in the wake of the success of Fearless, we're continuing to refine
how League is played at the highest level. We've been exploring an improvement
to side selection in order to give players
and teams more agency in the draft. Yeah, so after detailed discussions with regional leagues, pro teams
and our gameplay team, we've decided to decouple side selection
and draft order  through a new approach we're calling “first selection”. Here's how it works: Teams who previously had the right of side
selection will now have the right of first selection. That means they can choose either
which side to play on, blue or red, or whether to pick first
or second in the draft. Once that choice is made,
their opponent can choose the other. So if team A chooses to play on blue
side, team B gets to decide whether they're going to draft first or second,
or if team A chooses to draft first, then team B gets to decide
which side they'll play on. This decoupling gives pro teams
more strategic flexibility to shape their approach based on their comfort, their preparation,
their opponent scouting whatever, rather than having to rely on the baked
in advantage that is tied to color. And our goal here is simple. We want to empower teams to make meaningful choices that
reflect their strategies and preparation, while also helping to mitigate
side imbalance through a competitive decision making
rather than through hardcoded advantages. We consider this a minor
but targeted update, one designed to complement Fearless Draft,
not redefine it. We'll continue to test, monitor
and collect feedback from players and fans as the Season unfolds. Ensuring that this new approach drives
the right kind of competitive balance and depth that we're aiming for. As we continue refining how competition evolves, it's just as important to honor the players who have shaped
League Esports into what it is today. Hall of Legends
will return later than usual this year, but fans can still expect its comeback
ahead of Worlds 2026. We'll have more to share
in the months ahead, but we can promise that our third inductee
is another true icon who has left an unforgettable
mark on our esports community. Well, that's a wrap on the latest updates
from LoL Esports. We're looking forward
to another incredible year of competition, and we'll have more to share soon
about our upcoming global events and future updates. So to every player, every team,
every fan around the world, we wish you the best of luck
in the 2026 Season. Let's make it another year to remember
for League of Legends Esports. Before we go, we want to talk about
some longer term plans for League after 2026. We're often asked if we're going
to make League of Legends 2 someday. And the answer to
that has been and remains: no. We've always been committed to constantly improving
and updating League from new game modes and champion updates to balancing the game
every other week, and so on. There are some things though, that do make the most sense
as part of a larger bundle of changes. And with that in mind, we've been working
on some bigger updates to League. We're not quite ready to show
you our work in progress. It's coming along well, but
it's just not ready for a first look yet. We want to tell you
about some of those things that, at this point, the majority of the League
team are now working on. So for starters, we're making
a brand-new around game client that's fully integrated
with the in-game experience rather than being a separate
application like today. We're also revamping Summoner's Rift
with entirely new visuals and a bit of new gameplay, plus some changes to Runes
and how you make some pre-game choices. And we're overhauling the new player
experience so that once we're done, it should be the best time ever
to get your friends into League. There is a bit more to this update than we’ve mentioned here, and we'll have more to share sometime
later this year between MSI and Worlds. Until then, we'll still of course have regular
dev updates on everything else going on. Your love and dedication to League is
what drives us to make this game better. Your voices and input are vital to League
and will help shape where we go next. So as always, thank you so much for your time,
your support and just for everything. Good luck, have fun and we'll see you on the Rift. Thank you all. Bye bye.

titre: League Destroyed Everyone's Expectations!
transcription: I do not believe Riot could have hoped for a better outcome. And thus concludes the final episode of the Jinxy series, which might not actually be the final episode. Who knows what's going to happen with this guy? But already we know that this guy has been a big blessing for this community because already the history of League of Legends has been rewritten because with his bold decision to run his own League of Legends tournament, even as a new guy, records were broken and his event went bigger than even Riot's own biggest events. So, yesterday was pretty wild because if you weren't paying attention to what's happening to suddenly see League of Legends being at the very top of the entire internet really was a big surprise to say the least. And that is exactly why the entire Ink Shadow Mastery situation happened in the first place. Because in case you missed it, after Jinxy announced that he would be running his own League of Legends tournament, which included some of the biggest names around, including Mr. beast. Of course, Riot noticed what was happening and they decided to give the tournament its exclusive Twitch drop. Specifically, for watching 30 minutes, you would get a special mastery emote. And for watching for 2 hours, you would get the entire legendary skin, Ink Shadow Mastery E, which up to this point was unheard of. All we ever got before were some capsules or maybe Hexte chests. But suddenly there is a new guy who starts his own tournament and suddenly Riot gives him big drops. Well, that's exactly the criticism that this got. There were people who were mad at Riot for giving Jinxy such a big support because previously they haven't really done that. Now, from personal experience, I've seen that even the smaller tournaments always get something from Riot to either give away to the community or to the players. But truth be told, it was never as big as this. So, at least partially, this criticism is totally valid. And that's also because, as I mentioned last time, Riot is working on the new Riot partner program where they are specifically focusing on being able to do cool things such as this. So, unfortunately, we will have to give right some more time, but eventually, who knows? Maybe everyone will have their own legendary drop. Likely not, but we can hope. In fact, uh Tyler actually put it the best himself. W Riot. Yeah, we had a lot of people asking us about um well, if they don't ever help us, I'll say this. Riot is now is a lot different than they were before. I think they're trying nowadays because I think they have to. If I think I would do like TCS, even Allstars, Riot has been wanting to help with Allstars, but usually when you get help from Riot, there's always like something you got to do, right? >> So, simply said, times change. And because League of Legends is not the monolith it once used to be, right is in a situation where they just have to try new things. And so in Jinxy's case specifically, they absolutely made the right decision because even before the tournament started, Jinx's viewership reached about 350,000 views, which was his own alltimes record. But far more importantly, there is also the overall statistic. Because sure, I understand not everyone is a Jinxy enjoyer. Truth be told, most of us didn't really know about him until this event. But with the combined power of all the big names who are part of this tournament, eventually if you had a look at League of Legends, they would be sitting at over 800,000 views. Now, yes, uh not everyone was actually part of the tournament. There still had to be a big portion of this viewership that was just people playing League, but even so, this is a bigger number than Riot can pull off on some of their bigger events, which is exactly why Riot pulled off the Ink Shadow Mastery drop. It doesn't matter that a lot of the viewers were here only for the skin. The truth is, people will remember the final number. And as I mentioned last time, that number is something that Riot can put onto their portfolio. Because even if all the other events burn, Riot has a new statistical proof that shows everyone that people are still interested in League of Legends and that under the right circumstances, it can pull off incredible numbers. And again, sure, this wouldn't be possible without Riot support. And Jinxy even addressed it himself. >> Bro, chat, I'm not going to lie. I said this 20 times, but we've done so many tournaments on so many. And as I said that, Rag get the 50 W right with the 50 gifted chat. We've done so many tournaments on so many different games, and not a single game has ever reached out about putting drops onto a tournament. League is the first game to do it, bro. So, W League. If there was not Drops, I would not have had 300K. That is insane. Wag, bro, for real. Chat, we're about to start uh basically everybody's late. >> And so now we may be wondering, how is this going to change the ecosystem around Riot since it was confirmed that the community can go wild on its own, which comes at the exact cost of 0 unless Riot wants to support them directly? Well, wouldn't it make sense for Riot to kind of refocus on the community a little bit more? Sure, they still have to run their own big events. you don't want to throw your entire LCS and LEC and whatever else under the bus, but at the same time, it would be silly to ignore what just happened here. Especially because what just happened here brought up some really funny clips. >> Press V. Go to the top lane and press V on that top W. Okay, THIS IS GOING TO BE REALLY HARD. titre: I'm coming back.
transcription: Well, Eugene, once you sign this, you're officially going to be a content creator for Team Liquid. Thanks, Steve. I'm really excited to do this. Me, too. When you know you have went >> [music] >> and failed so many times, you maintain the mentality I'm going to take this [music] and do better next year. So, we're going to get Bjergsen and Wippo and Santorin and Hojbot and CoreJJ. Okay, they're probably going to make Worlds at least, right? I don't think that happened. Really? Well, at least tell me you didn't forget about the time that I saved the puppy from the burning tree with Yorn during the fire thunderstorm earthquake cyclone. Oh, so that was crazy. I guess you're right. You have been there for all the big moments. Yeah, let's make some more big moments together. Ah, good to see you, man. Yeah, you smell good, man. Hey guys, it's Eugene, aka Poabelter. I'm here today because I'm rejoining Team Liquid as a content creator. So, looking back on it, Team Liquid was the team that when I played on it, we had a really successful run. Probably the most fun that I had as a professional. The best that I got along with my teammates. [music] So, it was kind of a no-brainer to join again. As someone that's been in the industry since I was like 14, and this [music] year I'm turning 30, you see a lot of people come and go, but there's guys like Steve who I've known since pretty much the very start. When I was 14, I used to [music] play on a team with Steve, and he's still here 15 years later. Dodo as well was someone who was a manager on [music] one of the early teams I was on. So, it's really comforting to see old friends. It's really great to also be working with Alienware who's really [music] invested in North American League of Legends, and it was always really good to work with them and and use their peripherals. So, hopefully I can just make [music] content that is really exciting and fun to fans. Thank you so much for the fans that show up every single day that I stream and are subscribed to my YouTube channel and stuff like that. I couldn't really do [music] any of this without your guys' love and support. So, thank you guys so much. Thank you so much to Alienware. Thank you to Team Liquid. [music] Thanks to you guys for watching this video all the way to the end, and stay tuned to see cool videos with me in it.

titre: I Played 100 Hours of League of Legends... Here's What Happened
transcription: picture this a complete Noob decides to play one of the hardest most competitive and occasionally toxic games out there for 100 hours what do you think would happen will he have a great time let's fing go or will he re-evaluate his life choices most importantly will he regret streaming the whole Nightmare on Twitch while his chat repeatedly tells him to get out while he still can hey there my name is Fallout and I decided to play 100 hours of League of Legends as a complete beginner this game has a reputation for being crazy difficult to learn and having a community that maybe doesn't like to sugarcoat things how will I feel at the end of 100 hours well there's only one way to find out within the opening minutes of my very first match I realized just how much I would need to learn if I ever wanted to get even a little bit good at this game from the moment I first booted up the game there were 165 champions in the game each with their own unique abilities spells and styles of play it became pretty clear that my first goal for the next few hours wouldn't be so much getting good as it would be learning not to freaking drown selected runes have been reforged select your creative a what I don't know what that means for my first game I chose Darius because I'll be honest I opened up a random tier list and it said he was good he also looks like a bit of a badass so why not completely unsurprisingly my team got destroyed I didn't understand the shop how to Forfeit and why the enemy Towers seemed to hate me as much as they did yeah to put it lightly it was a show but the Lost didn't discourage me level up hey that's something right over the course of the next hour or so I got the ball rolling and I began to understand more and more as the games went by I even managed to get the joy of my very first enemy Champion kill in my second game that definitely got me pumped oh dude I slay an enemy an En has oh let's go but shortly after I was brought back down to earth as I learned just how devastating an AFK teammate in League can be dude what are you doing are you AFK oh my God that explains a lot in League you earn XP and gold as the game goes on which allows you to become more and more powerful throughout the match if someone is AFK for the whole game and comes back it doesn't matter how good of a player they might be they'll be bringing a pointy stick to a gunfight in short it's sucks and I went into future games just praying that all of my teammates would actually show up around 2 hours in I guess I'm beginning to get the hang of things a bit at least I'm getting kills that being said I'm also getting obliterated just a bit too often for my likings why asked one of my friends who's a seasoned League veteran if she could take pity on me and give me a little coaching and guess what it worked I learned about some pretty important stuff like health potions huh yeah you heard me it took me 2 and 1/2 hours to figure out a basic starting item eventually I decided to unlock a new character Garen who apparently has no Mana bar meaning one last thing for my dumbass to worry about he also likes to spin around the room more than a dizzy toddler so that was fun yeah get it's been about 3 hours and I feel like I'm beginning to understand the basics but apparently there's something really important which I haven't even bothered to learn yet my chat has been yelling at me about this unbinded camera feature and my chat never ever gives me bad advice so I say what the hell and give it a go never have I wanted to puke so quickly with a visual change it's awful I hate it and can't imagine that people would willingly play this way I will never conform to whatever this is around 5 hours in I get my first win when the enemy team forfeits and that is a damn good feeling even if I'm kind of playing like a man with no fingers I decide that I need some extra practice so I start playing against the AI for a little bit just to get a better feel for the characters I'm beginning to learn after 7 hours I can tell for sure that I'm still way in over my head but slow progress is being made something else I'm understanding is that this game is annoyingly addictive losing feels like getting salt thrown right into your eyes but winning is an absolute rush just before hour 16 I have the league Rush equivalent ENT of drinking a dozen shots of pure caffeine I didn't know it at the time but this would end up being maybe my first memorable match at first things are not going well we're getting our asses kicked and at this point I've seen enough League to know that if it starts badly it's probably going to end badly too it felt like the enemy team was always just one step ahead of us we get so close to making some awesome plays but end up getting shot down in flames just before we can pull them off I get taken out but my teammates managed to avenge me what happened next can only be described as a new player counter push wet dream I got to tell you nothing beats the crazy feeling of marching through everything standing in your way with your team taret priority buddy oh let's go dude oh my God more they're still respawning except for her what's she going to do about it yeah for real March in you you want go taking everything IDE we're not taking everything here we take this and yeah take this and run take this and run y we out we out we [Laughter] out let's go shortly after our amazing Counterattack we use our new found confidence to lock up the wi and for all my hype and supposed awesome team play I'm rewarded with a D+ we have the greatest comeback of the day and I still get a D+ [ __ ] yeah turns out at the end of each game every player receives a rating based on their performance I sure as hell don't know what the highest rank you can get is but I damn well want to find out I also know that D+ probably ain't that great as it's explained to me though the highest ranks don't just depend on who gets the most kills or who has the highest creep score and to make it even more complicated rank requirements are different for each role in the game at this point I at least know the names of each role but not much else better to not worry about it right now and return to getting my ass beat like a drum the next few hours went by surprisingly quickly I played some pretty tight games including one where we were this close to getting reamed but we managed to hold out and win oh my gosh we deci oh minions kill this guy get him get him get him get him get him oh my God destroy everything let's go my God vict I learned something pretty important about Wards around this time it turns out you can actually place them at range instead of just dropping them at your feet that's a thing yes I was double digigit hours into playing when I learned that and yeah I did go to college at this point I felt like playing with someone who I can try to learn from again so I called in the big guns YouTube Legend and part-time male model Mr fruit now I'm no stranger to content creator collaboration but this was no joke as I soon found out fruit goes hard in League nice Not only was he doing well in virtually every game we played together but he managed to to do it with multiple different Champions me on the other hand yeah not so much the final game we played together was particularly embarrassing I got massively humbled meanwhile Mr fruit over here playing likey moonlights as a member of T1 it was so impressive to see someone who was actually really good at the game perform it kind of lit a fire under me it also made me realize just how far away I truly was from the skill ceiling in League over the course of the next 10 hours or so I decided to go start playing solo and as painful as was it was clear that I was at least getting a little bit better as I pushed forward still I knew I couldn't do this whole challenge in one go so I decided to take a tiny break from league Here's an overview of how coach comms works. Each team may assign one coach per game submitted with the starting roster. Coaches can be changed between games. Only a head coach, assistant coach, or analyst is eligible. Sub players are not allowed. Use of the coachcoms is optional. Here's how coach coms operates during matches. The coach is placed in an isolated room wearing a soundproof headset to ensure competitive integrity. They may only view the five player POVs on stage. No live stream, spectator mode, or external devices are permitted. Each team has three coach comms uses per game with 45 seconds per use. Unused users do not carry on. After all users are exhausted, the coach may leave the room. If they leave early, they cannot return. Team members not participating will have access to standard spectator mode for postgame review. Coach Comms has received positive feedback from teams and fans and adds an additional strategic layer to the game. The feature will be reviewed at the end of Split 2. Stay tuned for more updates. point that they thought she could not possibly be a problem Riot had no choice they had to do something a couple of months later Yumi would receive a gameplay update fixing Yumi forever just kidding of course it didn't Riot had made some interesting choices with this update previously yumi's passive had worked in a way where you had to detach from her Ally in order to attack the enemy and create a shield the new Yumi passive allowed her to activate her passive while she is still attached to an ally she could just land any ability instead of having to Auto attack what Riot did effectively was remove the very small bit of counterplay that Yumi actually had previously it was possible to stun Yumi when she detached to activate her passive now that was no longer possible so you simply couldn't interact with Yumi whatsoever the biggest complaint with Yumi up until this point was that not being able to Target her was unfair and frustrating with this update they made it so that you could literally not interact with her at all this change further cemented the things that people hated about Yumi she was already criticized for being basically a permanent buff that you could play as now that's what she literally was previously yumi's kit did have some skill expression you could hop off of your ally to land your passive you could block some damage and then reattach there was no incentive to do this anymore her skill floor was now even lower and so was her skill ceiling one of the pain points of yumi's design was that the best thing for Yumi to do was to attach to the strongest champion and stay there this meant that Yumi would often abandon a weak 80 carry and attach to a Bruiser instead to try to stop this Riot added a system where Yumi would need to stay attached to one person to get more value out of her abilities so now if your Bot Lane Lost Lane you had two Champions doing nothing instead of one the rework was not well received by the community and make it easy to introduce new players to a difficult genre by designing Yumi in this way though they made it so that the Yumi player barely even got to do anything the rework just made this problem even worse Yumi still exists in this state to this day with minor changes Buffs and Nerfs and she is still hands down the most hated champion in the game this is a graph from over 2 years ago showing what people voted as the most hated champion and it speaks for itself this is a thread from 3 months ago asking players what Champion they felt did not belong in the game nothing has changed and the Yumi update just made people dislike Yumi even more it is definitely true that MOBA games in general are very intimidating and overwhelming for new players it isn't surprising that Riot tried to bridge the gap it's tragic that the champion That was supposed to help new players get into the game ended up being the most hated and annoying Champion that Riot has ever created and that is really saying something I'm curious what do you think Riot should do about Yumi is her design fixable or is it so fundamentally flawed that she should just be removed from the game before finishing up I titre: The Beautiful Disaster of Old League of Legends
transcription: The year is 2009. Demon Souls, Modern Warfare 2, Minecraft, and Left 4 Dead 2 are all huge hits. But, at the same time, a new game called League of Legends is taking the gaming landscape by storm. This new game had no competitive scene. When it officially launched, there wasn't even a ranked mode. It only had 40 champions, and it was absolutely fantastic. It was perfect. Back then, it wasn't League of Legends the esport, it was the game that you played with your friends for the sole purpose of having fun. But, Riot removed that OP mechanic many years ago. Because the game wasn't competitive, the game's lack of balance was seen as more of a feature rather than a frustrating problem to be solved. If you're old enough to have been playing this game 15 years ago, you're probably already having traumatic flashbacks to this. In this video, we're going to look at the broken old designs of champions that you either forgot or made yourself forget as a defense mechanism. This is the nightmarishly broken golden age of League. Before esports existed, before anyone cared about balance, back when League really was just a game. But first, let me introduce you to this video's sponsor, Surfshark. Let's say that you're at a cafe, and right there in the middle of the day, you get hit with a sudden need to play League of Legends. Luckily, you have your laptop with you because you're supposed to be studying. But wait, public Wi-Fi is dangerous. Your information would be at risk. Well, you won't have to worry about that because with Surfshark, you're protected and free to go 0 and 9 while using your laptop touchpad. Online security is very important, and with Surfshark, you can use one account for your entire household, so everyone benefits. They even have an alert feature as part of the Surfshark One bundle, which will notify you if they detect that your data has been leaked online. My favorite part is that the Surfshark team is so confident that you'll love the product that they offer a 30-day money-back guarantee. So, there's no better time to try it out. They even have 24/7 customer service. If you'd like to try it out, you can use my link, surfshark.com/myth, in the description below. Thanks again to Surfshark for sponsoring this video, and let's jump back into it. The first champion we have to talk about is Jax. And to talk about him, we'll also need to talk about one of League's most broken mechanics of all time. The version of Jax that existed when League released was a balance nightmare. To start with, his passive. He received an additional 15 to 25% of the AD and AP that he gained from items. Starting off strong. He still had his Q Leap and his W Empower to buff his next auto attack, but the real problem was his E. It passively increased his dodge chance. The dodge stat allowed you to dodge auto attacks, empowered auto attacks, turret shots, and even do this. There were lots of ways to get more dodge back in the day, like Ninja Tabi and Phantom Dancer. But that's not all. Since we're talking about the golden age of League, we have to talk about rune pages. Runes were bought with influence points, which was the game's old currency. Each rune gave you bonus stats from the start of a match, and they made an enormous difference in how strong a champion was early on. There were even scaling runes for late game champions. Using the right rune page could easily make the difference between destroying in a match up or getting absolutely crushed. Runes were so important that without them, a lot of junglers just couldn't jungle. They would just die to their camps. With the most expensive tier three dodge runes, Jax could start the game with 11% dodge chance, not including his E passive. Then from masteries, which used to look like this by the way, he got another 2% dodge chance. Jax could then, just for fun, build six Phantom Dancers, each giving another 20%. With all of these stacked together, including his E passive, which at one point gave 24%, he could have a total of 82.6% chance to dodge any attack. You're probably thinking, "Wow, that means Jax's E was pretty strong." And it was, but that's only the passive part. Once he dodged an attack, Jax could activate his E for the next 7 seconds. When activated, he dealt AoE damage while stunning all enemies for 1 second. This also used to have a 100% AP ratio, which was nerfed to 80% when the game actually released. Plus, it had a base cooldown of 4 seconds. So, when fighting Jax, it was possible to be stunned 25% of the time while also being burst for basically his total ability power every 4 seconds. And that's not factoring in any cooldown reduction. You're probably realizing that the counter play to Jax was to not auto attack him and to instead rely on spells to burst him down. For example, a burst combo from a mage. And technically, that was the counter play, but it was much easier said than done because we still haven't talked about his ultimate. Each basic attack granted him bonus attack speed, stacking 10 times. Hitting the same unit twice made the next attack deal bonus magic damage. Then, when activated, Jax gained a flat amount of magic resistance scaling with his dodge chance. So, built into Jax's kit was a way to counter his own counter, scaling with what countered everything else. When Jax was eventually reworked in 2012, he was very balanced and was never again a problem for Riot. The next champion to talk about might surprise you because she's often forgotten in these old discussions. Tristana, specifically full AP Tristana. If you didn't know that AP Tristana was a thing back in the day, the thumbnail for this old montage tells you pretty much everything that you need to know. At a glance, Tristana's old kit wasn't too different from what we have now. Her passive gave her bonus attack range based on her level, but unlike now, it did not increase the range of her E or her ultimate. So, you could be in range to attack someone, but not hit them with any of her abilities, meaning that it was easy to accidentally misposition resulting in instant death. Her Q increased her attack speed. Her W was her rocket jump. It wasn't very different except for one specific thing. Instead of the 50% AP ratio that it has now, it had a 100% AP ratio. Her E on the other hand was very different. It was a point and click ability that caused damage over time with an 80% AP ratio while also applying a healing reduction effect. If she got the killing blow on any unit, she dealt AoE damage just like now. This version of her E was super obnoxious in lane. Unlike the current version that forces her to commit to a trade to deal damage, back then she could just drop it on an enemy and walk away. You can already see the potential of AP Tristana just based on her W and E, but the real damage was in her ultimate. It looked the same as it does now, point and click, deal damage, knock the enemy away, but it had an additional 50% AP ratio for a total of 150. It also had 150 extra range. Prior to League's official release, the AP ratio was actually as high as 200%. Tristana stayed in this state with these AP ratios for years until she was eventually reworked in January 2015. Any game with AP Tristana in it was like playing a horror game. Whenever you were anywhere remotely close to a bush, there was always a chance that you get jump scared. The next champion to talk about has pretty much always been meta from the very beginning but in many different roles. The character who could one-shot you with a projectile that was very often invisible from over a screen length away. Nidalee. The good old days when that was enough. Her Q though was really the only thing that she needed. But, her spear was the real reason to pick her. Unlike today, Nidalee couldn't access her cougar form at all until she put a point into it at six. It also used to provide a passive 10% dodge chance. Riot loved to just sprinkle a little RNG here and there. Even Sivir had dodge chance, but only while moving. Even ignoring that the crows could bounce at all, Fiddlesticks could still crowd control any target for a total of 5 seconds every 10 to 15 seconds using point-and-click spells. Basically, rest in peace to any carry who got in range of Fiddlesticks in a fight, [music] and good luck killing a carry that was protected by Fiddlesticks. Speaking of weird old champions, though, Soraka used to look like this, and believe it or not, her model was one of the least weird things about her. Soraka's W was a point-and-click heal that also provided a huge armor buff, at one point granting 125 at max rank. She could also use her heal on herself. Even from rank one, it only had a 10-second cooldown. Soraka's E was honestly ridiculous. It was a point-and-click ability which granted up to 250 mana to any ally she casted on, completely for free. In addition to that, when cast on an enemy, it silenced them for up to 3 seconds at max rank. There's a good reason that you don't see Riot give champions point click silences anymore and why so many were removed over the years. The mechanic is super frustrating and incredibly strong. Soraka's E was so hard to balance that it was changed almost non-stop throughout League's early years. At one point it gave half the mana to an ally and half the mana to Soraka or she could cast it on herself to receive the full amount. And finally, Soraka's ultimate. Being an infinite fountain of sustain for her and her team meant she was pretty boring to play against and considered a very obnoxious support. The secret truth about Soraka though is that she was much more than just a frustrating support. She was also an absurd laner. Urgot also received up to 130 armor and magic resist for the duration, later changed to 50% damage reduction. He was really going to need it, too. While it may seem like a bad idea to have an ability that drops your ADC into the middle of the enemy team, what you need to remember is that Urgot was not really an ADC. And that meant the counterplay to Urgot was to just not get hit by his E, which brings us to the other half of this nightmare bot lane combo, which made it impossible for Urgot to ever miss his E. Taric. To start with, his original passive restored mana equal to a percent of his damage dealt. His Q was a point and click heal, with each auto attack reducing its cool down by up to 3 seconds. But, there were some, like one of the most annoying old champions to play against, Akali. They were so important that her early laning phase depended entirely on them. Part of her kit didn't even work without them. And this was all because of her passive, which reads like a 2026 Riot Games banger. The first part made it so that when she gained 10 attack damage, she gained 10% spell vamp. For every five ability power after that, she dealt 1% more. The idea was that she would get new effects and scalings based on the bonus stats of the items that she bought. But, by running a specific rune page, she would activate both parts of this passive from level one. Having the right runes was literally the difference between starting with 10% more damage, 10% more spell vamp, both of them, or neither. A full page of the cheapest runes would cost about 8,600 influence points. A full page of tier three runes could cost up to 30,000 influence points, depending on the runes you were buying, which was enough to buy about five of the most expensive champions. And there were runes for basically everything, even energy regen. Let's not forget the scaling version of each rune, too. This would occasionally cause posts from players who felt that the rune system was kind of pay-to-win. For most players, it wasn't realistic to be able to afford both new champions and runes. Instead, players had to choose between either buying new champions to play or buying runes so that you weren't at a disadvantage from the start of the game. As a result, most players chose to just have a couple of standard rune pages. One for AP champs, one for AD champs, and if you were an Akali player, one for her, too. Overall, it's for the best that rune pages were removed, even if the 1% crit rune is remembered very fondly. But, back to Akali. While in the area, she gained bonus armor and magic resist and entered stealth. It also slowed any enemies in the area. Her E dealt damage around her. That's it. And finally, her ultimate. People complain about champions having too many dashes in 2026, but in 2010, old Akali had three of them on one ability. In lane, Akali used her Q to either poke or last hit every 6 seconds. The old Akali combo involved throwing a Q on someone, waiting until the mark was about to expire, then triggering it with an auto or alt auto. Then you would Q again and instantly auto or alt for a ton of burst damage. In lane, because she had three charges on her ult, it was very difficult to play safe. She could just jump to your caster minions and then on to you. Having no skill shots to speak of meant that there was also very little counterplay to her. Either she had enough damage to just annihilate you with a combo, or she didn't. Her W, though, did have a lot of counterplay. Just drop a pink ward, and Akali would be revealed. When playing against Akali, it was pretty much mandatory to keep pink wards ready for when she tried to enter into a fight. She's a perfect example of a champion that was feast or famine, and she was ultimately reworked due to how frustrating she was to play against. are tons of myths and misconceptions around farming the biggest myth about farming is that having high CS numbers means being good at csing in the laning phase getting insane Farm leads at actually mainly comes from macro in the mid game let me explain 1 minion wave is worth 125 gold on average that is until the 15-minute Mark where 1 minion wave is worth 147 gold on average meaning they become 18% more valuable this is because at the 15minute Mark Siege minions begin spawning every two waves instead of every three increasing the average gold value and finally at the 25-minute Mark each minion wave becomes worth 195 gold on average again this is because Siege minions now spawn on everying single wave that's a 33% increase in gold now while minions themselves become worth more gold you as a champion actually outscale them too you get more items which means more damage which means you can clear those waves even faster and you'll have boot upgrades meaning you can move faster to take other things on the map here's an easy way to visualize this so every 30 seconds a wave spawns in the early game if you last hit two waves perfectly you would get 12 minions in 1 minute however later on in the mid game you can clear waves and move so fast you could clear one wave wave then go and farm nearby camps and get back to the next wave or you could clear one wave then rotate to an empty Lane to pick up the wave there both of those scenarios allow you to potentially double that 12 minions in 1 minute to 24 it gets even crazier cuz when Towers fall it means you can move up the lane is pushed to your side you can push that out farming at an accelerated rate by moving up the lane while you clear waves basically what this means is that when it comes to farming the early game simple rules in the mid game are the main reasons why Talia ended with so much more Farm than carus all right and now that you understand those two rules let's get into the tactics you'll be using in the mid game so let's say you're following the empty Lane Rule and starting your push the ideal goal is to push the wave to the enemy's Tower remember this is what causes a threat you're not only threatening to both take the tower but also for those minions you push to be denied by dying to the tower itself if the enemy sends no defender or a weak Defender well you always want to look to punish by either taking the Tower or Tower diving however once the outer towers are down you do have to be more cautious over extending remember to track your team's mat pressure and the enemies when your team has no pressure and enemies are missing well you want to default do pushing into farming jungle camps and if there's no nearby camps you can't go wrong simply recalling to spend your gold in these spots however if you're pushing a wave and the the enemy sends a Defender you can't Tower dive and it prevents you from taking the tower too well then check your map if you see that your teammates do have pressure well that's your signal to do what's called a push into RAM by pushing first you pin an enemy down giving you a temporary number Advantage when you roam that being said you don't force it if a winning fight isn't there for example before when Tristana pushed Bond into farming she ran out of nearby camps and so transitioned into a Roman mid however remember that number Advantage is temporary so once JY and Nami finish catching that wave they're now mid and the fight is now a number disadvantage for Tristana but just remember the rules there's now an empty Lane bot so off we go keep in mind tristana's team is literally forcing a 3v5 here over a rift Herald while there's two empty lanes mid and top and jungle camps top side that's fine and will happen all the time in Solo Q as long as you're the one pushing that empty Lane you get massively fed from the minion waves and Towers you can pick up and so there you go all you need to do is follow the first two rules one farm when your team has no pressure and two push empty lanes once you're pushing those Lanes you just cycle between between three tactics one if the enemy has no defender or a weak Defender push to take the Tower or Tower dive two if the enemy has an actual Defender and your team has no pressure then push into farming camps head to Another Empty lane or simply use it as a recall timing and three if the enemy has an actual Defender and your team has pressure will then push into roaming for a number Advantage fight could be a good idea don't force it though if there isn't a good fight there just head to the next empty Lane usually it's the lane you were just in from the wave being pushed back now I want to address the three most common issues and pain points players have when it comes to what you just learned the first one is that they can't do this because as soon as they go off to farm or pick up a wave their team just forces a fight and dies it's a misconception that the winning team fight is worth more than pushing an empty Lane for example in that previous Tristana clip the enemy team here got one kill that's 450 gold from the kill and assist and then a rift Herald that's 200 gold Tristana at the start of the sequence has 9,300 total gold she then ends it with 10,400 she just gained 1,100 gold and that's it's not even shared it's all funneled on her it's not even close so don't make the mistake of thinking your team dying while you push is bad you'll actually be incredibly farmed and in a great position to carry if it keeps happening now the second thing players say is that they can't push a lane because some fed assassin like Evelyn or nocturn just deletes them there's two solutions to this firstly and some of you might not like to hear this but this is actually because you need to improve your early game if the majority of your games involve you behind multiple enemies by the mid game then you have mistakes you need to fix prior to that point those types of Champions won't be a threat if you're entering the mid game as the strongest player in the game still I fully realize that's not always achievable every single game even if you play Perfect sometimes your teammates feed so hard your own lead won't be enough to compensate that's where our second answer comes in it's okay to die to them in fact it can sometimes be your best play for example in this game the enemy cidan is fed and cidan is a pretty tough matchup for most 80 carries and yet you'll see me go into a s Side Lane against him get baited into thinking he roamed or Rec called and quickly gray screen here's the thing notice how as I die my team gets a winning fight tops side the reason why this works works is that when someone is sent to defend you that is stronger than you well that almost always means that if you had grouped a team fight and they did two well they would have had more of an impact in that fight by being ahead by dragging a Fed enemy to the sideline it pulls them away from objectives and team fights giving your team a way back into the game at the same time you'll see once I respawn and head to push again Cid in groups mid to team fight instead cool that means we get a ton of free farm from the empty Lane rule which then immediately forces Cid in back bot preventing him from being able to do something after he won that team fight recall and back to the S Side Lane again pulling cast in you can start to see how I'm starting to get strong enough to potentially win the fight and outplay him in this case I even pull lease in and Lux too this is fantastic it's delaying the game as the enemy isn't able to take as much off the map with their lead as they're constantly being delayed by having to go bot and deal with me and once I respawn back bot we go putting down pressure cassid in the most fed member matches us and again we nearly kill him we keep getting stronger from farming those empty lanes in the meantime we lured cassid and Bot put him low so we asked to recall and Jinx spots side this not only again delays the game but gives our teammates a number advantage that results in two kills top side into then actually getting a pick on the ciden it's like an invisible passive buff you give your teammates to increase the odds of them winning fights by continuing to delay the game this way and getting ourselves farmed it will inevitably lead to us just being a terrifying threat in the sidelane being able to Now kill pretty much every Defender and often what happens is that the enemy just gets so frustrated not knowing how to end the game they start forcing fights like this ciden and one misstep in the late game means super long death timers and that means an end to the game I should also mention the reason why delaying the game like this is so effective is due to the concept of gold inflation in League having early gold leads are much more impactful because they represent a larger percentage of the total gold in the game for example if someone has 6K gold and you have 3K gold for a 3K gold lead on you at 10 minutes well that means they're 100% more powerful than you in terms of items but if that same person has a 3K gold Advantage at 25 minutes when you have 13k gold and they have 16 well now they're only 16% more powerful than you and it goes without saying when everyone is full build then gold leads don't even matter at that point as you can't convert that extra gold into items long story short win behind early just by delaying the game even if you don't shorten the gold lead or even if it gets worse it's still an improvement due to how gold inflation works and finally the third most common paino players have is they say they can't go push a sid Lane because their teammates will flame them spam pinging blame them for lost team fights and even intentionally feed or AFK okay so first there's no way to have a high win rate in Solo que with without your teammates flaming you I have taught and played this game for over 10 years I've done countless climbs through lowo with absurd win rates and every time I'm flamed nearly every game I play that's why I personally just mute everyone as soon as the game starts and just play my own game and recommend you do the same when you start using strategies that will actually get you to climb out of the rank you're stuck in players in your games will naturally start flaming you for doing something different than them but news flash that's why they're stuck at that rank they don't know how to get out of the rank they're in and that's why what you're doing is confusing to them so why would you listen to the calls of the players you're trying to climb past additionally to climb in solo queue you have to adopt a carry mentality yes your teammates will die fall behind feed the enemy but the whole idea is that you're generating so much gold on yourself and getting so strong that it won't matter you'll 1 v9 carry the game anyways okay but what about when they literally start intentionally feeding running it down or just leave the game here's the thing no one solo queuing can achieve a 100% win rate no matter how good they are you need to adjust your mentality and expectations during your climb you will have unwinable games no matter how good you play no matter how perfect you play a very reasonable expectation you should develop is that around one out of three of the games you play will be unwinable you don't know which games those will be though so you still have to be levelheaded and try your best no matter how doomed the games feel and I know losing one in three sounds like a lot but that would still mean you'd have a 66% win rate which is incredibly high and makes for a very fast climb here's the thing though League actually has pretty high short-term variants when it comes to solo qu what this means is that even if you were using a strategy that had a true win rate of 66% even over 100 games you could encounter a win rate variance of like 7% meaning two players using the exact same strategy at the end of 100 games one of them could have a 59% win rate one of them could have a 73% win rate just due to luck the more games you play though the less the statistical variance and the closer you're going to get to your true win rate there's a reason why they call it grinding solo que it really is a grind so adjust your expectations to fit reality trust the process keep a good mentality and the results will come with time and if you want to speed titre: Twilight’s End | 2025 Season 3 Cinematic - League of Legends
transcription:
Tu ne devrais pas être ici, Sénéchal. Les mortels ne devraient pas
interférer avec le pouvoir des dieux. Est-ce vraiment ce que vous êtes ? Des dieux ? Je ne suis pas la réponse. Je ne suis que le mal,
encore et toujours. Le mal ne renonce pas au pouvoir. Tu aurais dû savoir que tu ne pourrais
pas te cacher éternellement. Nul ne revient de par-delà le voile. J'ai vu par-delà le voile, j'ai vu l'esprit des Terres premières s'éveiller dans toute sa splendeur. Et puis tout est parti en fumée
en un instant. Yunara est tombée, et les défenses du temple
ont été anéanties. Il ne reste plus personne. Ton pouvoir plongera le monde
dans les ténèbres, que tu le veuilles ou non. Tu n'as aucune idée de ce que tu exiges. Je sais exactement ce que j'exige. Tu as tout sacrifié pour sauver ce monde par le passé. C'est à mon tour, désormais. Je ne peux le contenir ! Même les démons peuvent faillir…
